faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stadium-specific visual designs; accurate graphical recreation of real MLB stadiums

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Catcher's-eye view perspective showing batter, pitcher, and field
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player control of batting; pitcher delivery animations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Field perspective from pitcher's mound with batter visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pitcher control mechanics; ball trajectory visualization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Real MLB team rosters (1993-1994 season data) displayed with licensed player names and team identities
Accurate graphical recreation of specific MLB stadiums with distinctive architectural features

environmental_storytelling.example: Each real MLB stadium is graphically recreated with specific visual characteristics, creating location identity and historical baseball context
Display of actual 1993-1994 MLB player names and teams communicates real-world baseball authenticity and competitive context
Jerry Coleman's play-by-play narration from San Diego Padres broadcasts provides authentic sports media atmosphere

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Wind-up animation; ball release timing visual feedback
Stance positioning; swing arc execution in response to player input
Positional movement toward ball trajectory; fielding animations based on ball location

notes: World Series Baseball is a sports simulation with minimal danger/safety/political signals. The game's manifestation layer relies primarily on team identity through licensed MLB properties, stadium authenticity, player roster accuracy, and perspective-based visual communication of game state (catcher's-eye view). No faction conflict, danger systems, or political world-states present. Visual communication is pragmatic and rooted in real-world baseball authenticity rather than symbolic world-building.
